flashcards.back: Establishes that there shall be a Governor for each state (amended in 1956 to allow one person to serve as Governor for two or more states).
Grants pardoning powers to the Governor of a state (excludes death sentences and court-martial punishments).
Deals with the appointment, tenure, responsibility, and oath of the Chief Minister and other State Ministers.
Provides for the appointment and qualifications of the Advocate General of the State.
Empowers Parliament to create or abolish a State Legislative Council upon a resolution passed by the State Legislative Assembly.
Specifies the composition of the State Legislative Assembly (Max 500, Min 60 members).
Specifies the composition of the State Legislative Council (Max 1/3rd of Assembly, Min 40 members).
Empowers the Governor to promulgate ordinances when the state legislature is not in session.
Defines the Gram Sabha as a body of all registered voters in a village area.
Mandates not less than one-third reservation of total seats for women in Panchayati Raj Institutions.
Mandates the constitution of a State Finance Commission by the Governor every 5 years.
Establishes the State Election Commission for conducting local body elections.
Constitutional executive head of a state; holds office during the pleasure of the President; minimum age 35 years.
Real executive head of the state government; leads the Council of Ministers and acts as the chief link to the Governor.
Highest law officer of a state; must be qualified to be a High Court judge; appointed under Article 165.
Independent constitutional authority appointed by the Governor; removable only in the manner and grounds of a High Court Judge.
First committee to recommend the 3-tier Panchayati Raj system (Gram Panchayat, Panchayat Samiti, Zilla Parishad).
Recommended a 2-tier Panchayati Raj system (Zilla Parishad and Mandal Panchayat) and political party participation.
Reviewed rural development and famously described grassroots bodies as 'grass without roots'.
First major committee to strongly recommend constitutional status for Panchayati Raj institutions.
Examined Centre-State relations, issuing landmark guidelines on Governor appointments and Article 356.
Constitutionalized rural local government, adding Part IX and the Eleventh Schedule.
Constitutionalized urban local government, adding Part IX-A and the Twelfth Schedule.
Contains 29 functional items devolved to Panchayati Raj Institutions under the 73rd Amendment.
Contains 18 functional items devolved to Urban Local Bodies under the 74th Amendment.
Gram Sabha is the full voter assembly (direct democracy); Gram Panchayat is the elected executive committee.
Village Level (Gram Panchayat) -> Block Level (Panchayat Samiti) -> District Level (Zilla Parishad).
Nagar Panchayat (transitional areas) -> Municipal Council (smaller cities) -> Municipal Corporation (metropolitan cities).

flashcards.front: Minimum Age to Contest Local Elections
flashcards.back: 21 years for contesting elections to Panchayats and Municipalities.
Hailed as the 'Magna Carta' of local self-government in colonial India, laying the foundation for municipal boards.
